prompt_text: PostHog vs Mixpanel for developer-led product teams
response:
# PostHog vs Mixpanel for Developer-Led Teams

## Quick Comparison

| Aspect | PostHog | Mixpanel |
|--------|---------|----------|
| **Setup** | Dev-friendly, self-hosted option | Easier initial setup, cloud-only |
| **Cost Model** | Usage-based, generous free tier | Usage-based, more expensive at scale |
| **Data Ownership** | Own your data (self-hosted) | Third-party hosted |
| **Core Strength** | Product analytics + feature flags | User behavior analysis |
| **Learning Curve** | Steeper for non-technical users | Gentler |

## PostHog Advantages

**For developers:**
- **Self-hosted option** — keep data in-house, no data residency concerns
- **Open source** — customize and audit the code
- **Developer experience** — excellent SDKs, API docs, local testing
- **Feature flags** — integrated A/B testing and experiments
- **Better free tier** — 1M events/month free (vs Mixpanel's limited free)
- **Session replay** — included, not an add-on

**Ideal if you:**
- Want engineering control over analytics infrastructure
- Need data privacy/compliance (HIPAA, GDPR locally controlled)
- Run experiments frequently
- Have small-to-medium event volumes

## Mixpanel Advantages

**For product teams:**
- **User journey focus** — excellent funnel and retention analysis
- **Faster time-to-value** — less setup overhead, works out-of-box
- **Non-technical UX** — product managers can self-serve queries
- **Proven at scale** — battle-tested for high-volume tracking
- **Mobile expertise** — stronger mobile analytics legacy

**Ideal if you:**
- Prioritize ease of use over data ownership
- Need sophisticated behavioral cohorts
- Focus on retention/churn analysis
- Have dedicated product/data analyst roles

## Potential Pain Points

**PostHog:**
- Self-hosted requires DevOps overhead
- Smaller community (vs Mixpanel)
- UI less polished for non-technical users
- Pricing can be opaque at high volumes

**Mixpanel:**
- Higher costs at scale (can be 3-5x PostHog)
- Less control over data
- Feature flags/experiments are premium add-ons
- Feels bloated if you only need core analytics

## Real-World Decision Framework

Choose **PostHog** if:
- You have 2+ engineers who'll own analytics infra
- Data privacy is a requirement
- You experiment frequently
- Budget is tight

Choose **Mixpanel** if:
- You have a dedicated product/analytics hire
- You prefer managed services
- You already use their ecosystem
- You won't self-host

## Middle Ground Options

- **Amplitude** — Similar to Mixpanel, better data exploration
- **Segment + warehouse** — Maximum flexibility, more DevOps work
- **Plausible/Fathom** — Privacy-first, but simpler analytics

**For most developer-led teams starting out:** PostHog offers better ROI and control, but requires slightly more technical setup upfront.
